Records.authors: Bauberger, S., Berends, Frits A., Bohm, M., Buza, M.
Records.description: Two-loop self-energy diagram with four massive propagators containing a one-loop bubble subdiagram (propagators 3,4) inserted on an internal line. Small $p^2$ result as quartic generalized hypergeometric series in $D$ dimensions, large $p^2$ via Mellin-Barnes. Imaginary part via complete elliptic integrals in $D=4$. One-dimensional dispersion integral representation derived.

Two-loop two-point four-propagator sector with four distinct internal masses, used in Sec. 4 as the efficiency benchmark. The paper focuses on reducing integrals of the form $I_{1,1,1,1,0,-r_1,-r_2}$ with two ASP numerators ($l_1 \cdot R$, $l_2 \cdot R$) for $r = r_1+r_2 = 1..6$, comparing FIRE6-only reduction to the iterative ASP formula followed by FIRE6 (Table 1). No master integrals or analytic results are computed; the paper reports CPU timings only. Note: the paper's Eq. (4.1) contains a typographical error listing two $z_4$ labels; the second is $z_2 = (l_1-p_1)^2 - m_2^2$. Called the '2-loop 3-point sub-sector example' in the paper.
Records.location: Section 4 (Comparison of efficiency), Eqs. (4.1)-(4.2) and Table 1

Records.description: Two-loop self-energy topology $S_3$: triangle graph with one edge doubled (bubble), giving 4 massive propagators. Has $1/\varepsilon$ UV divergence. Also covers topology $S_5$ (at $s=0$) and tadpoles $T_1$ (at $s=0$). Used for Higgs self-energy computation via sector decomposition. Called $S_3$ in the paper.

Records.authors: Martin, Stephen P., Robertson, David G.
Records.description: Two-loop self-energy integral with four propagators forming a bubble-triangle topology in $d=4-2\varepsilon$. Denoted $U(x,y,z,u)$ in the TSIL basis. The triangle connects three vertices with a doubled edge between two of them, creating the second loop. Symmetric under $z \leftrightarrow u$.
